n.description: Number of vertices in the perfect graph

m.description: Number of edges in the perfect graph

E.description: Edges of the graph; each row lists the two endpoint vertices of an edge, with the smaller vertex index listed first

P.description: Number of disjoint clusters partitioning the vertex set

C.description: Binary cluster membership matrix: C_{i,p} is 1 if vertex i belongs to cluster p, 0 otherwise

Q.description: Number of maximal cliques of the graph

L.description: Number of vertices in each maximal clique

K.description: Vertex indices belonging to each maximal clique of the graph

assumptions.description: The number of vertices is positive.
The number of edges is non-negative.
Each cluster contains at least one vertex.

code.python: assert all(sum(C[i][p] for i in range(n)) >= 1 for p in range(P))

assumptions.description: The clusters partition the vertex set: every vertex belongs to exactly one cluster.

code.python: assert all(sum(C[i][p] for p in range(P)) == 1 for i in range(n))

assumptions.description: Every edge connects two distinct, valid vertex indices.

code.python: assert all(0 <= i < j <= n - 1 for i, j in E)

assumptions.description: The graph is perfect: every induced subgraph has chromatic number equal to its clique number.

code.python:
G = nx.Graph()
G.add_nodes_from(range(n))
G.add_edges_from(E)
assert nx.is_perfect_graph(G)

assumptions.description: All vertex indices in K are valid.

code.python: assert all(0 <= i <= n - 1 for q in range(Q) for i in K[q])
assert all(len(set(K[q])) == L[q] for q in range(Q))

assumptions.description: Each K_q forms a clique: every pair of distinct vertices in K_q is connected by an edge.

code.python:
adj = [[False] * n for _ in range(n)]
for i, j in E:
    adj[i][j] = adj[j][i] = True
assert all(adj[K[q][a]][K[q][b]] for q in range(Q) for a in range(L[q]) for b in range(L[q]) if a != b)

assumptions.description: K covers every clique of the graph: since K enumerates all maximal cliques, every clique (maximal or not) is a subset of the vertex set of some K_q.
assumptions.formulation: \forall S \subseteq \{0,\ldots,n-1\} \text{ a clique}, \ \exists q \in \{0,\ldots,Q-1\}: S \subseteq K_q

x.description: Equals 1 if vertex i is selected, 0 otherwise

t.description: Estimate of the number of colors needed to color the selected vertices

constraints.description: Exactly one vertex is selected from each cluster.

code.gurobipy:
for p in range(P):
    model.addConstr(gp.quicksum(C[i][p] * x[i] for i in range(n)) == 1)

constraints.description: t is at least the number of selected vertices within any maximal clique of the graph; since the graph is perfect, the largest such count over all maximal cliques equals the chromatic number of the induced subgraph on the selected vertices.

code.gurobipy:
for q in range(Q):
    model.addConstr(t >= gp.quicksum(x[i] for i in K[q]))

objective.description: Minimize the number of colors needed to color the selected vertices.

notes: Reformulates Model 2 of Seker, Ekim, and Taskin (2021) as a static MILP.

The paper's Model 2 bounds t below by chi(G[x]), the chromatic number of the subgraph induced by the selection x; this is not a linear expression, so the paper resolves it with cutting planes t >= sum_{i in K^(j)} x_i generated iteratively from a maximum clique K^(j) of G[x^(j)] found at each step of a cutting-plane algorithm, rather than a single static formulation.

Since the input graph is perfect, chi(G[x]) = omega(G[x]) (clique number) for every selection x, and omega(G[x]) equals the size of the largest maximal clique of the full graph G that lies entirely within the selection. We therefore replace the paper's dynamically generated cuts with the full, static set of constraints t >= sum_{i in K_q} x_i for every maximal clique K_q of G, precomputed once from the input graph in gen_params.py.

Q, L, and K are derived parameters computed from the base graph parameters (n, m, E) via maximal-clique enumeration; m and E are carried through from problem.json unchanged, while Q, L, and K are specific to this formulation and not part of problem.json.
